item.title: Carnot Theorem
item.description: TL;DR The Carnot Theorem states that there can never be an engine, operating between a two reservoirs, that has an efficiency that is higher than the Carnot Engine working between the same two reservoirs.

item.title: Effective Temperature and Non-Equilibrium States
item.description: TL;DR This notes discusses the notion of effective temperatures and non-equilibrium states in open quantum systems, particular in the context of Quantum Heat and Work - TLS Example.

item.title: Open Quantum Systems and Thermalization
item.description: TL;DR Once we have an understanding of how to separate quantum work from quantum heat, we are required to address the mechanisms of heat exchange in quantum systems that are interacting with the surroundings.

item.title: Quantum Heat and Work - TLS Example
item.description: TL;DR This note describes a very simple example of a quantum system (a TLS, spin-1/2 particle) interacting with an external, uniform magnetic field.

item.title: Quantum Heat and Work
item.description: TL;DR Here, we try to extend the classical thermodynamic concepts of Heat and Work to Quantum Mechanical systems.

item.title: The Lindblad Master Equation
item.description: TL;DR The Lindblad Master Equation, or formally the Gorini-Kossakowski-Sudarshan-Lindblad (GKLS) equation, is the fundamental equation of motion for open quantum systems.

item.title: What is a Superoperator?
item.description: TL;DR An explanation of superoperators, like the Liouvillian that shows up in the general density matrix equation of motion When we move from the usual wavefunction formalism to the density matrix formalism, we move from a vector (1D) to a density matrix (2D).

item.title: Heat Engine
item.description: TL;DR A thermodynamic heat engine is a theoretical (and practical) construct that extracts macroscopic usable work from the miscroscopic thermal energy of a system.

item.title: Quantum Engines and Refridgerators, Cangemi et al
item.description: TL;DR Engines are systems and devices that convert one form of energy into another - typically a more useful form that can perform work.

item.title: Stimulated Emission
item.description: TL;DR Stimulated Emission is an interesting phenomena, where a system decays from an energetic state to a less energetic state due to an external photon coupling to it.
